title: Safe or Harmful for Magnets? — Sorting Tool
instructions_for_student: Drag each action card from the top deck into the correct bin. 'Safe for Magnets' takes actions that preserve magnetic strength; 'Harmful for Magnets' takes actions that weaken or destroy it. A correct drop locks the card green and shows the reason. A wrong drop bounces the card back with a hint.

cards.reason: Heat destroys the alignment of magnetic domains.
cards.hint: High temperature disrupts the internal structure that makes a magnet work.

cards.reason: Unlike poles facing keeps magnetism intact.
cards.hint: Storing in pairs with unlike poles on the same side preserves magnetic strength.

cards.text: Drop the magnet on the floor
cards.hint: A sudden impact can scramble the tiny magnets inside, weakening it.

cards.reason: Wood spacer prevents poles from cancelling each other.
cards.hint: A non-magnetic spacer keeps the stored pair from demagnetising each other.

cards.reason: Hammering destroys the ordered magnetic domains.
cards.hint: Like dropping, a hard blow breaks the internal magnetic order.

cards.text: Keep magnets away from mobile phones
cards.reason: Strong fields near electronics cause mutual damage.
cards.hint: Keeping magnets away protects both the magnet and sensitive devices.

cards.text: Put iron pieces across the ends
cards.reason: Soft iron keepers complete the magnetic circuit.
cards.hint: Iron pieces placed across the poles act as keepers and preserve strength.

cards.reason: Magnetic fields can damage electronic components.
cards.hint: Remote controls have sensitive electronics that magnets can disrupt or destroy.

agent_readme.description: Drag-and-drop sorting tool for Grade 6 Science — Chapter 4.5 Fun with Magnets / magnet care. Eight action cards are shuffled and displayed in a deck. Students drag each card into one of two bins: 'Safe for Magnets' or 'Harmful for Magnets'. Correct drops lock the card green with a reason; wrong drops animate the card with a shake and show a contextual hint. A confetti summary screen appears when all cards are sorted.

interaction_model.drag_mouse: HTML5 draggable API — onDragStart, onDragOver, onDrop on bins
interaction_model.drag_touch: Custom touch handlers (onTouchStart/Move/End) for mobile; reads elementFromPoint to detect active bin under finger
interaction_model.correct_drop: Card locked into bin with green border + reason text. mstGreenPop glow animation. Score increments.
interaction_model.wrong_drop: Card shakes (mstShake). Hint panel appears above the card. Card stays in deck for retry.
interaction_model.completion: When all cards are placed, summary view slides in with confetti, two-column breakdown (Safe / Harmful), and Play Again.

cards.description: Action cards shown in the drag deck

item_fields.text: string — short action label shown on card
item_fields.reason: string — shown inside bin after correct placement
item_fields.hint: string — shown above card after wrong placement

additionalProps_schema.showHints: boolean (default true)  — show hint tooltip on wrong drop
additionalProps_schema.showReasons: boolean (default true)  — show reason text inside bin after correct drop

responsive_notes.deck: flex-wrap row — cards wrap naturally on narrow viewports
responsive_notes.bins: flex-wrap row, each bin flex: 1 1 240px — stacks to single column below ~520px
responsive_notes.font_sizes: clamp() for card text and header; readable down to 320px
responsive_notes.touch: Full touch drag support via custom handlers; no external library needed

customisation_examples.note: Replace cards array with new id/text/icon/correctBin/reason/hint objects. Any even count works; odd counts are fine too.
Add more objects to the cards array. Deck wraps automatically.

safe_actions: Keep magnets away from mobile phones
Put iron pieces across the ends
harmful_actions: Drop the magnet on the floor
chapter_mapping.source: NCERT Curiosity Grade 6 Science, Chapter 4 — Exploring Magnets, Section 4.5 Fun with Magnets / Magnet Safety callout (pp. 73)
